name: listAgentPools
description: List all agent pools. Lists all agent pools with pagination support

limitPerPoolType.description: Maximum number of agent pools returned

after.description: The cursor to use for pagination. It is an opaque string that specifies your current location in the list and is obtained from the `Link` response header. See [Pagination](https://developer.okta.com/docs/api/#pagination).

name: listAgentPoolsUpdates
description: List all agent pool updates. Lists all agent pool updates

poolId.description: ID of the agent pool for which the settings apply to

scheduled.description: Return only scheduled or ad-hoc updates. If this parameter isn't provided, Okta returns the entire list of updates.

name: createAgentPoolsUpdate
description: Create an agent pool update. Creates an agent pool update

poolId.description: ID of the agent pool for which the settings apply to

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: getAgentPoolsUpdateSettings
description: Retrieve an agent pool update's settings. Retrieves the current state of the agent pool update instance settings

poolId.description: ID of the agent pool for which the settings apply to

name: updateAgentPoolsUpdateSettings
description: Update an agent pool update settings. Updates an agent pool update instance settings

poolId.description: ID of the agent pool for which the settings apply to

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: getAgentPoolsUpdateInstance
description: Retrieve an agent pool update by ID. Retrieves an agent pool update by its `updateId`

poolId.description: ID of the agent pool for which the settings apply to

name: updateAgentPoolsUpdate
description: Update an agent pool update by ID. Updates an agent pool update instance and returns the latest agent pool update

poolId.description: ID of the agent pool for which the settings apply to

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: deleteAgentPoolsUpdate
description: Delete an agent pool update. Deletes agent pool update

poolId.description: ID of the agent pool for which the settings apply to

name: activateAgentPoolsUpdate
description: Activate an agent pool update. Activates a scheduled agent pool update

poolId.description: ID of the agent pool for which the settings apply to

name: deactivateAgentPoolsUpdate
description: Deactivate an agent pool update. Deactivates scheduled agent pool update

poolId.description: ID of the agent pool for which the settings apply to

name: pauseAgentPoolsUpdate
description: Pause an agent pool update. Pauses a running or queued agent pool update

poolId.description: ID of the agent pool for which the settings apply to

name: resumeAgentPoolsUpdate
description: Resume an agent pool update. Resumes a running or queued agent pool update

poolId.description: ID of the agent pool for which the settings apply to

name: retryAgentPoolsUpdate
description: Retry an agent pool update. Retries an agent pool update if the update is unsuccessful or communication with Okta was interrupted during an agent auto-update

poolId.description: ID of the agent pool for which the settings apply to

name: stopAgentPoolsUpdate
description: Stop an agent pool update. Stops an agent pool update

poolId.description: ID of the agent pool for which the settings apply to

name: listDeviceIntegrations
description: List all device integrations. Lists all device integrations for your org. Examples include Device Posture Provider, Windows Security Center, Chrome Device Trust, OSQuery, and Android Device Trust.

name: getDeviceIntegration
description: Retrieve a device integration. Retrieves a device integration by `deviceIntegrationId`

deviceIntegrationId.description: The ID of the device integration

name: activateDeviceIntegration
description: Activate a device integration. Activates a device integration and populates the related configurations by `deviceIntegrationId`

deviceIntegrationId.description: The ID of the device integration

name: deactivateDeviceIntegration
description: Deactivate a device integration. Deactivates a device integration by `deviceIntegrationId`

deviceIntegrationId.description: The ID of the device integration

name: listDevices
description: List all devices. Lists all devices with pagination support. >**Note:** To list all devices enrolled by a user, use the [List all devices endpoint in the User Resources API](/openapi/okta-management/management/tag/UserResources/#tag/UserResources/operation/listUserDevices). You can return a subset of devices that match a supported search criteria using the `search` query parameter. Searches for devices based on the properties specified in the `search` parameter conforming SCIM filter specificat…

limit.description: A limit on the number of objects to return (recommend `20`)

search.description: A SCIM filter expression that filters the results. Searches include all device `profile` properties and the device `id`, `status`, and `lastUpdated` properties. Searches for devices can be filtered by the contains (`co`) operator. You can only use `co` with these select device profile attributes: `…

expand.description: Includes associated user details and management status for the device in the `_embedded` attribute

name: getDevice
description: Retrieve a device. Retrieves a device by `deviceId`

name: deleteDevice
description: Delete a device. Deletes (permanently) a device by `deviceId` if it has a status of `DEACTIVATED`. You can transition the device to `DEACTIVATED` status using the [Deactivate a Device](/openapi/okta-management/management/tag/Device/#tag/Device/operation/deactivateDevice) endpoint. This request is destructive and deletes all of the profile data related to the device. Once deleted, device data can't be recovered. However, reenrollment creates a new device record. > **Note:** Attempts to delete a …

name: activateDevice
description: Activate a device. Activates a device by setting its status to `ACTIVE` by `deviceId`. Activated devices are used to create and delete device user links.

name: deactivateDevice
description: Deactivate a device. Deactivates a device by setting its status to `DEACTIVATED` by `deviceId`. Deactivation causes a device to lose all device user links. Set the device status to `DEACTIVATED` before deleting it. > **Note:** When deactivating a Device, keep in mind the following: - Device deactivation is a destructive operation for device factors and client certificates. Device reenrollment using Okta Verify allows end users to set up new factors on the device. - Device deletion removes the d…

name: suspendDevice
description: Suspend a Device. Suspends a device by setting its status to `SUSPENDED`. Use suspended devices to create and delete device user links. You can only unsuspend or deactivate suspended devices. > **Note:** `SUSPENDED` status is meant to be temporary, so it isn't destructive.

name: unsuspendDevice
description: Unsuspend a Device. Unsuspends a device by returning its `status` to `ACTIVE`. >**Note:** Only devices with a `SUSPENDED` status can be unsuspended.

name: listDeviceUsers
description: List all users for a device. Lists all Users for a device by `deviceId`

name: updateADGroupMembership
description: Update an Active Directory group membership. Updates an Active Directory group membership directly in Active Directory > **Note:** See **Before you begin: Active Directory integration with the following setup** in the [Use Okta Access Certifications to manage AD group membership](https://help.okta.com/okta_help.htm?type=oie&id=ad-bidirectional-group-mgt-configure) product documentation.

appInstanceId.description: ID of the Active Directory app instance in Okta

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: listPushProviders
description: List all push providers. Lists all push providers

name: createPushProvider
description: Create a push provider. Creates a new push provider. Each Push Provider must have a unique `name`.

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: getPushProvider
description: Retrieve a push provider. Retrieves a push provider by `pushProviderId`

name: replacePushProvider
description: Replace a push provider. Replaces a push provider by `pushProviderId`

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: deletePushProvider
description: Delete a push provider. Deletes a push provider by `pushProviderId`. If the push provider is currently being used in the org by a custom authenticator, the delete will not be allowed.

name: listRealmAssignments
description: List all realm assignments. Lists all realm assignments

limit.description: A limit on the number of objects to return

after.description: The cursor used for pagination. It represents the priority of the last realm assignment returned in the previous fetch operation.

name: createRealmAssignment
description: Create a realm assignment. Creates a new realm assignment

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: listRealmAssignmentOperations
description: List all realm assignment operations. Lists all realm assignment operations. The upper limit is 200 and operations are sorted in descending order from most recent to oldest by ID.

limit.description: A limit on the number of objects to return

after.description: The cursor to use for pagination. It is an opaque string that specifies your current location in the list and is obtained from the `Link` response header. See [Pagination](https://developer.okta.com/docs/api/#pagination).

name: executeRealmAssignment
description: Execute a realm assignment. Executes a realm assignment

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: getRealmAssignment
description: Retrieve a realm assignment. Retrieves a realm assignment

name: replaceRealmAssignment
description: Replace a realm assignment. Replaces a realm assignment

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: deleteRealmAssignment
description: Delete a realm assignment. Deletes a realm assignment

name: activateRealmAssignment
description: Activate a realm assignment. Activates a realm assignment

name: deactivateRealmAssignment
description: Deactivate a realm assignment. Deactivates a realm assignment

name: listRealms
description: List all realms. Lists all realms. > **Note:** The `search` parameter results are sourced from an eventually consistent datasource and may not reflect the latest information.

limit.description: Specifies the number of results returned. Defaults to 10 if `search` is provided.

after.description: The cursor to use for pagination. It is an opaque string that specifies your current location in the list and is obtained from the `Link` response header. See [Pagination](https://developer.okta.com/docs/api/#pagination).

search.description: Searches for realms with a supported filtering expression for most properties. Searches for realms can be filtered by the contains (`co`) operator. You can only use `co` with the `profile.name` property. See [Operators](https://developer.okta.com/docs/api/#operators).

sortBy.description: Specifies the field to sort by and can be any single property (for search queries only)

sortOrder.description: Specifies the sort order: `asc` or `desc` (for search queries only). This parameter is ignored if `sortBy` isn't present.

name: createRealm
description: Create a realm. Creates a new realm

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: getRealm
description: Retrieve a realm. Retrieves a realm

name: replaceRealm
description: Replace the realm profile. Replaces the realm profile

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: deleteRealm
description: Delete a realm. Deletes a realm permanently. This operation can only be performed after disassociating other entities like users and identity providers from a realm.

name: listAppServiceAccounts
description: List all app service accounts. Lists all app service accounts

limit.description: A limit on the number of objects to return

after.description: The cursor to use for pagination. It is an opaque string that specifies your current location in the list and is obtained from the `Link` response header. See [Pagination](https://developer.okta.com/docs/api/#pagination).

match.description: Searches for app service accounts where the account name (`name`), username (`username`), app instance label (`containerInstanceName`), or OIN app key name (`containerGlobalName`) contains the given value

name: createAppServiceAccount
description: Create an app service account. Creates a new app service account for managing an app account

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: getAppServiceAccount
description: Retrieve an app service account. Retrieves an app service account specified by ID

id.description: ID of an existing service account

name: updateAppServiceAccount
description: Update an existing app service account. Updates an existing app service account specified by ID

id.description: ID of an existing service account

requestBody.description: The JSON request body. Refer to the Okta API docs for the schema.

name: deleteAppServiceAccount
description: Delete an app service account. Deletes an app service account specified by ID

id.description: ID of an existing service account
